title: Al-Omari Mosque
description: One of the most important historic mosques in Fuwah, originally built in the Mamluk era and restored during the Ottoman period.

title: Al-Qanai Mosque
description: Famous for its hanging architecture and intricate wooden minbar, dating back to the Mamluk Circassian period.

title: Nasr Al-Din Mosque
description: Located on the Nile bank, historically significant as a lookout point, featuring a distinct minaret.

title: Abu Al-Makarim Mosque
description: Known for having unique architectural features and being a center for religious learning in old Fuwah.

title: Sidi Salem Mosque
description: An ancient mosque holding the shrine of the local Sufi figure Sidi Salem, reflecting the popular piety of the Delta.

title: Tekiyet Al-Khalwatiya
description: A rare surviving example of a Sufi retreat (Tekiya) in the Delta, used by the Khalwati Sufi order.

title: Rab' Al-Khataybeh
description: A historic inn or wikala used by merchants, showcasing the commercial importance of Fuwah on the Nile trade route.

title: Gate of the Tarabish Factory
description: Religious/Industrial landmark; the monumental gate of the Fez (Tarabish) factory established by Muhammad Ali Pasha.

title: Temple of Wadjet
description: Ruins of the great temple dedicated to the Cobra Goddess Wadjet, protector of Lower Egypt.

title: Statues of Sekhmet
description: Granite statues of the lioness goddess found at Buto, likely moved there by Ramesses II.

title: The Sacred Well
description: A complex water system used for ritual ablutions within the temple precinct.

title: Church of the Virgin Mary (Sakha)
description: Built on the spot where the Holy Family dwelt. Famous for the 'Bikha Isous' (Footprint of Jesus) stone.

title: Mosque of Ibrahim El-Desouki
description: One of the largest mosques in Egypt, housing the shrine of Ibrahim El-Desouki, a major pole of Sufism.

title: King Fuad Palace
description: A royal rest house built by King Fuad I, now used for administrative purposes but retaining its architectural grandeur.

title: Tabiet El-Fanar (Fort)
description: Coastal fortification built to defend the northern coast against naval invasions.

title: Old Burullus Lighthouse
description: Historic lighthouse structure on the Mediterranean coast.

title: Tell Qabrit
description: Archaeological mound containing remains of a settlement and necropolis near Fuwah.

title: Tell El-Khanziri
description: Large archaeological hill in the northern delta marshes, showing occupation layers from various periods.

title: Tell Mesto
description: One of the string of ancient settlements located in the Metoubes area.

title: Tell El-Nashart
description: Significant archaeological site believed to be ancient Phragonis.

title: Tell Sidi Salem
description: The mound upon which the modern town is partially built, containing ancient strata.

title: Tell Al-Kom Al-Ahmar
description: Red mound; archaeological site revealing red brick structures and pottery kilns.

title: Al-Daein Mosque
description: Another historic mosque in Fuwah known for its architectural style typical of the Delta Ottoman period.

title: Mosque of Hassan Nasr
description: Historic mosque located in the heart of the old city of Fuwah.

title: Hammam of Sakha
description: Remains of ancient bath structures found in the historic district of Sakha (Xois).

title: Tell Atshan
description: Archaeological site in the Burullus lake region.

title: Sidi Ghanem Mosque
description: Historic shrine and mosque located near the lake.
